questions.explanation: A galvanometer is an electromechanical measuring device used to measure the strength of current and direction in an electric circuit. An Ammeter, also called ampere meter, is a measuring instrument used to measure the magnitude of the electric current flowing in a circuit. It only measures the magnitude of current and not the direction. The electromotive force or EMF is the potential difference between two terminals (when no current is flowing) which is measured by a Potentiometer. A Voltmeter is an instrument used for measuring the potential difference, or voltage, between two points in an electrical or electronic circuit.

questions.q: The unit of potential difference is
questions.explanation: Charge: It can be defined as the fundamental property of a body which enables it to attract or repel other bodies and due to which it experiences a force when placed in an electric or magnetic field. A body can be positively (less no. of electrons) or negatively (more no. of electrons) charged. An American scientist Benjamin Franklin is credited to give the name of the charge as positive or negative. Opposite charges neutralize each other's effect. - Like charges repel each other and Unlike charges attract each other. - Charges on a body can be developed by direct contact or by the method of induction. When we rub an object such as straw or a piece of paper it gets charged this is an example of charging by contact and if we bring a positively charged rod near a metal surface, the electrons will get accumulated on the surface of a metal (as unlike charges attract each other) making it a negatively charged entity. This is called charging by induction. - If the size of the charged bodies is very small in comparison to the distance between the two, then those charged bodies will be called point charges. Charge is a scalar quantity and is additive in nature. Charges cannot be created or destroyed; it is conserved for an isolated system. Charges are quantized i.e, q=ne, where 'n' is an integer and 'e' is the basic unit of the charge and e=1.602192×10⁻¹⁹c. - (Quantization of charge means an electric charge will always be an integral multiple of 'e'). The SI unit of charge is coulomb ('c'). If 1 Ampere current is flowing through a circuit for 1 second then the value of charge flowing through that circuit is 1 Coulomb. Coulomb's law: - The force between the two-point charges is directly proportional to the product of their magnitude and inversely proportional to the square of the distance between them. - $F = \frac{1}{4\mu\epsilon} \times \frac{q_1 \cdot q_2}{r^2}$ Power: Power can be defined as the rate of transfer of energy per unit time or the rate at which a work is performed. The SI unit of power is Watt. Power is also measured in KWh or Horsepower (746 watt). Potential difference: It is the difference in potential across the two poles of a battery in an electric circuit. The SI unit of potential difference is Volt. Resistance: It is the property of a material which opposes the flow of current through it. 'Ohm' is the SI unit of Resistance and is denoted by the symbol 'Ω'. Ohm's law may be expressed mathematically as V/I = R.

questions.explanation: Kilowatt hour is the unit of energy. [Energy = Power Time] 1 KWh electrical energy is equal to 1 kilowatt (10³watt) power consumed in 1 hour.

questions.q: How many units of electricity will be consumed if you use a 60 W electric bulb for 5 hours every day for 30 days?
questions.explanation: Given, Power =60 watt, total time in hours = (5 h×30d) = (5 ×30) hours= 150 hours. Unit of electricity consumed is given in KWh (1 KWh=1 unit) So, [Energy = Power (in Kilowatt) × Time (in hour)] No. of units consumed will be = (60 ×150)/1000 KWh (divided by 1000 to convert power given in watt to kilo-watt) = 9000/1000 KWh = 9 KWh = 9 units. Hence, option B is correct.

questions.q: An AC current can be produced by
questions.explanation: Dynamo is an electric generator which generates electrical power by converting mechanical energy into electrical energy through electromagnetic induction discovered by Michael Faraday. - According to this principle, when a wire moves through a magnetic field, it causes electrons to move, which generates electricity. The simplest practical generator consists of a rectangular coil, known as an armature which rotates between the magnets. When the armature rotates, it cuts through the magnetic field produced by the magnets. This change in the magnetic field induces a voltage in the coil, causing an electric current to flow. The mechanical energy used to rotate the armature is thus converted into electrical energy. The current produced by a simple dynamo is alternating current (AC), as the direction of the current changes with each half turn of the coil. Dynamos can convert AC into DC by incorporating a component called a commutator. | Alternating Current (AC) | Direct Current (DC) | | --- | --- | | It is a type of electrical current, in which the direction of the flow of electrons switches back and forth at regular intervals or cycles. | It is a type of electrical current which flows consistently in one direction in a steady voltage. | | It is most commonly used in electric power for household equipment, office, buildings etc. | It is commonly used in electric devices that run on batteries. Example, mobile phones, flashlights etc. | | It was first tested based on the principles of Michael Faraday in 1832 using a Dynamo Electric Generator. | Italian physicist Alessandro Volta was the first to produce direct current in 1800. | | The frequency of AC is different in different country. But, generally, the frequency is 50 Hz or 60 Hz. | DC has no frequency or zero frequency. | | Alternating current is used to transmit electricity over long distances of less than 1000 km. | DC cannot be transferred over a very long distance. It loses electric power. |

questions.explanation: A power level of 1 horsepower (hp) is approximately equivalent to 746 watts (W) or 0.746 kilowatts (kW). Scottish Engineer James Watt invented the horsepower metric in the late 18th century to compare the output of steam engines with the power of draft horses.

questions.q: The paramagnetic theory of magnetism applies to

options.E: None of the above/ more than one of the above

questions.explanation: The paramagnetic theory of magnetism applies to those substances which when placed in an external magnetic field acquires a feeble magnetism in the direction of the magnetic field. These substances lose their magnetism when the external magnetic field is removed. Examples: Aluminum, Platinum etc. Info Bits: Ferromagnetic Substances are those substances which when placed in a magnetic field, are strongly magnetized when placed in the external magnetic field. Examples: Iron, Cobalt and Nickel. Diamagnetic Substances are those substances when placed in a magnetic field acquires feeble magnetism in the opposite direction of the magnetic field. Examples: Zinc, copper and silver.

questions.q: 'Ohm' is a unit of measuring
questions.explanation: 'Ohm' is a SI unit of measuring Resistance and is denoted by the symbol 'Ω'. Resistance→ The electrical resistance can be defined as the ratio of the voltage (applied to the circuit) to the electric current flowing through the circuit. It is the property of a material which opposes the flow of current through it. Ohm's Law: - Let 'V' be the potential difference between the ends of a conductor, through which a current I is flowing. Then, - According to Ohm's law Or V=RI, ----------------- (1) (Where the constant of proportionality R is called the resistance of the conductor.) Resistance depends upon the material as well as the dimensions of the conductor. Also, R = ρ (L/A), ---------------------- (2) Where R is the resistance, L is the length and A is the cross-sectional area of a conductor. here, ρ is the resistivity and it depends upon the temperature and material of a conductor but not on the dimensions of the conductor. The SI unit of resistivity is Ohm-meter. On putting the value of 'R' from equation (2) in equation (1), we get: - V = I × R = I × ρ (L/A). - I/A is called current density and is denoted by J. The SI unit of the current density is A/m². Unit of Voltage → 'Volt'. Voltage can be defined as the pressure exerted by the battery or power source in a circuit to drive the charged particle (electrons) through a conductor or a conducting circuit. In simple terms it is the difference in potential across the two poles of a battery. Unit of Current→ 'Ampere'. - Electric Current in a circuit can be defined as the rate of flow of charged particles across a conductor. I = Q/t, where Q is the charge and t is the time. SI unit= coulomb/ second= ampere. Unit of Conductivity→ Siemens per meter (S/m) or Ω⁻¹m⁻¹ or mho m⁻¹. - Electrical Conductivity can be defined as the amount of electric current passing through a conducting material per unit time. Conductivity is the reciprocal of Resistivity.

questions.q: The unit of electrical resistance of conductor is
questions.explanation: Resistance: It is the property of a material which opposes the flow of current through it. 'Ohm' is a SI unit of Resistance and is denoted by the symbol 'Ω'. R= V/I (where the constant of proportionality R is called the resistance of the conductor.) Resistance depends upon the material as well as the dimensions of the conductor. Capacitance: It is the amount of charge which can be stored by a capacitor at a given voltage. The unit of capacitance is Farad (Coulomb/Volt). - C=q/V - Where, C= capacitance. - q= amount of charge in coulombs. - V= voltage. 1 Farad= 96500 coulombs. Capacitor: A capacitor is a system of two conductors separated by an insulator. When we connect it to a voltage supply, it starts storing charges and when the voltage is disconnected the stored charge (is dissipated) can be used for the functioning of the electrical or electronic devices, thus it acts as a temporary battery. It is widely used in appliances of everyday use like - Mobile-chargers, AC Motor, Water-pump, Air-Conditioner, Fan etc to store charges. Reactance of a capacitor is inversely proportional to frequency.

questions.q: If the length of wire is doubled, its resistance will become
questions.explanation: If the length of wire is doubled, its resistance will also get doubled. R=ρ(L/A) From this formula, we can see that resistance of a wire is directly proportional to the length of the wire. So, putting L_new =2L in the above equation, we get: R_new = ρ (2L/A) = 2 ×R (as R= ρ (L/A)) Thus, upon doubling the length the resistance of the wire also gets doubled.

questions.q: On heating, the resistance of a semiconductor

options.E: None of the above/More than one of the above

questions.explanation: When a semiconductor is heated its resistance decreases. (When a conductor is heated its resistance increases). With increase in temperature, outermost electron acquires energy and thus greater number of bonds inside the semiconductor are broken. Hence a large number of electrons come out from those bonds. As a result the number of charge carriers increases leading to increase in its conductivity and consequently the resistance decreases. Info Bits: Semiconductor: Semiconductors are the materials which have a conductivity between conductors and insulators. These materials are used basically in the solid state electronic devices like diode, transistor, ICs, etc. Conductors (mostly Metals) have low resistivity; insulators have very high resistivity, while semiconductors have intermediate values of resistivity. Semiconductors are elemental (Si, Ge) as well as compound (GaAs, CdS, etc.).

questions.q: Which of the following is not based on the heating effect of current?
questions.explanation: When current flows through a conductor, heat energy is generated in the conductor, known as the heating effect of current. Except tube light all others contain a coil of wire. This coil of wire is called an element. When these are connected to the electric supply, they become hot and give out heat. The amount of heat produced in a wire depends on its material, length and thickness. Info Bits: Working of Tube light: The tube light also called the fluorescent lamp converts electrical energy into useful light energy. The tube light is a low-pressure mercury vapour gas discharge lamp that works on the principle of fluorescence to produce visible light. Edmund Germer who was a German inventor is recognized as the father of the fluorescent lamp. When the power button is switched on, the electric current passes through the tube. This current then passes through the gas and energizes the mercury vapour; this vapour delivers ultraviolet radiation through a discharge process. The radiation of ultraviolet rays causes the phosphor coating present on the inner wall of the lamp to radiate visible light.

options.B: depends on the current passed in the electrolyte
options.C: depends on the volume of the solvent in which the electrolyte is dissolved

questions.explanation: Faraday constant is a universal constant. Its value is 96485.33289 C/mol. It does not depend on the amount of electrolyte, the current in the electrolyte, and the amount of charge passed through the electrolyte. Info Bits: Michael Faraday: was an English scientist who contributed to the study of electromagnetism and electrochemistry. His main discoveries include the principles underlying electromagnetic induction, diamagnetism, and electrolysis. The SI unit of capacitance is named in his honour: the farad. Faraday Constant is the value that represents the amount or magnitude of electric charge carried by one mole of electrons. It was named after Michael Faraday and is denoted by the symbol F. And the unit used to measure the constant is Coulombs per mole (C/mol). One common use of the Faraday constant is in electrolysis calculations.

questions.q: When a bar magnet is placed in the earth's magnetic field, with its north pole pointing towards the geographical north, the number of neutral points around the magnet is

options.E: None of the above/More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Neutral Point: It is a point where the resultant magnetic field is zero. At this point, the horizontal component of the Earth's magnetic field is balanced by the magnetic field due to the magnet. When a bar magnet is placed in the earth's magnetic field, with its north pole pointing towards the geographical north, the number of neutral points around the magnet is 2. The point at which the resultant magnetic field is zero is called neutral point or null point. Since the net magnetic field is zero at neutral points, the compass needle remains unaffected.

questions.q: Electrically charged particles from space travelling at speeds of several hundred km/sec can severely harm living beings if they reach the surface of the Earth. What prevents them from reaching the surface of the Earth?

options.A: The earth's magnetic field diverts them towards its Poles
options.B: Ozone layer around the earth reflects them back to outer space
options.C: moisture in the upper layers of atmosphere prevents them from reaching the surface of the earth
options.D: None of the statements A, B & C given above is correct

questions.explanation: Electrically charged particles from space traveling at a speed of several hundred km/sec are diverted by the earth's magnetic field towards the pole. Info Bits: We know that the earth behaves as a magnet and hence produces invisible magnetic field lines which start from the south pole of the earth, curve around in space and converge at the north pole of the earth. These magnetic field lines surrounding the earth form the magnetosphere. This magnetosphere acts as a shield and deflects most of the electrically charged particles coming from the Sun. When a moving charged particle enters into a magnetic field, they experience a force called Lorentz force which is acting in the perpendicular direction to both the magnetic field as well as the moving charge particle, as a result the charged particle gets trapped in the earth's magnetic field and follow a helical path along the field lines and gets accumulated at the poles as the magnetic field lines come closer to each other at the poles. The density of these charged particles thus increases at the poles. When they collide with the atoms and molecules of the atmosphere, excited oxygen atoms of the atmosphere emit green light and excited nitrogen atoms emit pink light. This phenomenon is called "Aurora Borealis" in physics. The display of light of various colors in the polar region like Alaska and Northern Canada in the sky is due to this phenomenon. In space, this deflection of charged particles is important for controlling the beams of electrons that are used to create images on a television screen.

questions.q: The process of charging a conductor by bringing it near another charged conductor, without making any contact, is called

options.E: None of the above/More than one of the above

questions.explanation: charging is a charging method in which an object is charged without actually touching another charged object. The charged particle is held near a neutral or uncharged conductive material that is grounded on a neutrally charged material. When a charge flows between two objects, the uncharged conductive material develops a charge with the polarity opposite that of the charged object. Charging by conduction involves the contact of a charged object to a neutral object. Convection – The process in which transfer of heat is done by actual motion of matter, called Convection. This process of heat transfer is possible only in fluids. Convection can be natural or forced. Radiation - Process of heat transfer which needs no medium, it is called radiation.

questions.explanation: Minimum environmental pollution will be caused by Hydrogen. When hydrogen powered fuel cells are used in electric vehicles no harmful substances are produced. The by-product is water (H₂O) and warm air which does not pollute the environment. All other fuel like Coal and Diesel produces carbon dioxide upon burning thus pollutes the environment. Kerosene produces carbon monoxide upon combustion, which is a greenhouse gas thus contributing to global warming and hence, kerosene also pollutes the environment.

questions.q: An electron of mass M kg and charge e coulomb travels from rest through a potential difference of V volts. The final energy in joules would be?
questions.explanation: When a charge q moves through a potential difference V, the energy gained (or work done on the charge) is: Energy=q×V For an electron with charge e the energy is: Energy=eV (in joules). This energy becomes the kinetic energy of the electron (since it starts from rest).

questions.q: In the universe, what are pulsars?

options.D: Radio waves emitted by a star

questions.explanation: Pulsars are rapidly rotating neutron stars that blast out pulses of radiation at regular intervals ranging from seconds to milliseconds. Jocelyn Bell discovered the first pulsar on August 6, 1967. Rotating Neutron stars have strong magnetic fields. They emit high-energy beams from its north and south magnetic poles. When these beams are pointed toward Earth and flash across us, as the neutron star rotates, we see pulses. So, astronomers named these neutrons as pulsars. Formation of a neutron star- - When a supermassive star begins to die, it forms a red supergiant. - After that, these stars explode and either evolve into white dwarfs, or become supernovae. - After the supernova explosion, if the remaining core of the star has a mass less than about three times the Sun's mass, then it forms into a neutron star (if the remnant is more massive, it will collapse into a black hole). - Neutron stars are so named because they are composed primarily of neutrons, as most of the protons and electrons in the core of the star are crushed together and collapses to form neutrons under the incredibly dense conditions. - Most neutron stars are observed as pulsars. So, all pulsars are neutron stars, but not all neutron stars are necessarily pulsars. Why in the news? In June 2023, Pune based astronomers discovered two new Millisecond Pulsars (MSP) — rotating neutron stars — using an indigenously developed novel technique, which was applied during an ongoing sky survey conducted by the Giant Metrewave Radio Telescope (GMRT). Millisecond pulsars emit radiation every few milliseconds. Info Bits: The Chandrashekhar limit determines whether a star dies as a white dwarf, or would launch a supernova to create a black hole or neutron star. He calculated that stars with more than 1.44 times the mass of the Sun (now known as the Chandrasekhar limit) would not become white dwarfs, but would be crushed by their own gravity into either a neutron star or a black hole.

questions.q: Due to temperature variation along a conductor, potential variation occurs along it. This phenomenon is known as

options.E: None of the above/ more than one of the above

questions.explanation: Thomson effect is related to the 'emf ' which develops between two parts of the same metal, when they are at a different temperature. So, it is the absorption or evolution of heat along a conductor when current passes through it when one end of the conductor is hot and another is cold. Info Bits: Joule-Thomson Effect: The temperature of the fluid changes when it is forced to flow through an insulated valve from a higher-pressure region to a lower-pressure region. This effect is known as the Joule-Thomson Effect. The Joule-Thomson effect is also known as Kelvin-Joule effect or the Joule-Kelvin effect. Peltier effect: When an electric current is passed across a junction between two materials then a temperature difference is created- one junction becomes cooler and one junction becomes hotter. It is the reverse phenomenon of the Seebeck effect. Seebeck effect: The phenomenon in which a current (or 'emf ') is produced in a circuit containing two or more different metals (thermocouple) when the junctions between the metals are maintained at different temperatures, is called the Seebeck effect. Note: In the question, it is mentioned "along a conductor," which means it is talking about a metal and not a junction; hence the correct answer is "Thomson effect" and not "Seebeck effect".

questions.q: If the doors of the refrigerator are left open for a few hours then the room temperature will be

options.D: Decrease only in the area in the vicinity of the refrigerator.
options.E: None of the above/more than one of the above

questions.explanation: The reason is that with the door open, the temperature will start to rise inside the refrigerator. The thermostat will kick in and try to cool it back down. This means the motor is running, which means heat is being added to the room.

questions.q: Food gets cooked faster in a pressure cooker because

options.A: Water starts boiling at low temperature due to high-pressure
options.B: Water starts boiling at high temperature due to high-pressure
options.C: Water starts boiling at low temperature due to low-pressure
options.D: Water starts boiling at high temperature due to low-pressure
options.E: None of the above/more than one of the above

questions.explanation: Boiling is a bulk phenomenon; boiling point is the temperature at which pressure exerted by the surroundings equals the vapour pressure of the liquid. Since there is a direct relationship between pressure around the surroundings and the boiling point of a liquid, an increase in pressure will increase the boiling temperature and it will decrease with the decrease in pressure. Hence due to increased pressure inside the pressure cooker food gets cooked quickly at a higher temperature. While at a higher altitude, lower pressure lowers the boiling point hence, food gets cooked slowly. It is to be noted that boiling happens at a fixed temperature while evaporation happens at any temperature. Evaporation is a surface phenomenon.

questions.q: In a pressure cooker, the temperature at which the food is cooked depends mainly upon which of the following?
questions.explanation: The variation in the size of the hole in the lid will vary the amount of outflow of steam from the pressure cooker. [size or area of the hole in the lid ↑, outflow of steam ↑ , the pressure inside the pressure cooker ↓ and Pressure ∝ Boiling point of the liquid, so temperature inside the pressure cooker ↓ and vice versa]. Weight of the lid contributes to maintaining the pressure inside the pressure cooker, [as the Pressure ∝ Boiling point of the liquid so, if the weight of the lid ↑, P ↑, temp ↑ and vice versa]. Hence, statements 1 and 3 are correct. Note: Variation in the temperature of the flame will have an impact only on the cooking time as cooking inside the pressure cooker depends upon the boiling point of the liquid, which is fixed for a liquid. Hence, statement 2 is incorrect.

questions.q: It takes much longer to cook food in the hills than in the plains because

options.B: due to low atmospheric pressure on the hills, the water boils at a temperature higher than 100
options.C: In the hills the atmospheric density is low and therefore a lot of heat is lost to the atmosphere
options.D: In the hills the humidity is high and therefore a lot of heat is absorbed by the atmosphere leaving very little heat for cooking.

questions.explanation: In the process of cooking, food particles need to be heated at a particular temperature for an ample amount of time to get them cooked. At higher altitudes, pressure gets decreased and so does the boiling point of the water which means water gets boiled at a lesser amount of heat provided, but the food does not get cooked in the same interval, so it takes longer to cook food on the hills. Thus, on the hills the atmospheric pressure is lower than that in the plains and therefore water boils at a temperature lower than 100° C, causing an increase in cooking time. [Refer to Q. No. 5 for more details].

questions.q: The poorest conductor of heat among the following is

options.E: None of the above/More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Among metals, Lead is the poorest conductor of heat. It is to be noted that heat conduction has everything to do with the lattice structure; it has nothing to do with electrons. Electricity conduction has to do with electrons. Silver is one of the best conductors of heat and electricity as it has a closely packed lattice structure (suitable for heat conduction) and it also has free electrons (for electricity conduction). Note: Diamond is a good conductor of heat but a bad conductor of electricity while graphite is a good conductor of heat and electricity both.

questions.explanation: Celsius and Fahrenheit are two temperature scales. The Fahrenheit and Celsius scales have one point at which they intersect. They are equal at -40°C and -40°F. Fahrenheit to Celsius formula represents the conversion of degrees Fahrenheit(°F) to degree Celsius(°C). The conversion formula for Celsius to Fahrenheit is: C/5 = (F-32)/9 where "C" is Celsius and "F" is Fahrenheit.

questions.q: Water drops do not stick to the oily surface

options.A: due to lack of adhesive force
options.C: because they cannot mix with each other
options.D: because water is lighter than oil
options.E: None of the above/More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Water drops do not stick to the oily surface due to lack of adhesive force. Info Bits: Adhesive force and cohesive force are two kinds of forces of attraction. Cohesive force is a force of attraction between molecules of the same substance while adhesive force is a force of attraction between molecules of different substances. Example of cohesive force: rain falls in the form of droplets rather than in the form of a fine mist because the cohesive force between molecules of water is very strong. Example of adhesive force: Water is poured on clean glass spreads and forms a thin, uniform film over the glass surface, it is because of the strong adhesive forces between water and glass. Note: The adhesive force between oil and water is less than the cohesive force of water, but higher than the cohesive force of oil. So, when the water is dropped on oil surface, its drop occupy spherical shape by shrinking, while when oil is dropped on water surface, it spreads widely in form of the thin film.

questions.q: Why is a small liquid drop spherical in shape?
questions.explanation: Small liquid drops are spherical due to surface tension. Info Bits: Surface Tension is described as a force per unit length acting perpendicular to the surface's tangential line. It is caused by uneven attractive forces that are acting downward and seeks to minimize a liquid's surface area. Its SI unit is Newton/metre The value of surface tension of any liquid depends upon its nature, temperature and the medium present on another side of the liquid surface. Surface tension decreases with rise in temperature and becomes zero at the critical temperature. If the liquid has negligible weight, its shape will be completely spherical. For example - rainwater drops and soap bubbles because drops occupy that form, in which its potential energy is minimum. Note: Hot soup is more tasteful than the cold one because the surface tension of the hot soup is less than that of the cold and so spreads over a larger area of the tongue.

questions.q: Detergents clean clothes in washing machine because water solution has
questions.explanation: Substances that can be used to separate grease, dust and dirt sticking to a surface are called detergents. When added to water detergents lower its surface tension due to additional intermolecular interactions. A detergent for which the capillary rise is minimum (or the one that causes maximum lowering of surface tension), is said to have a better cleansing effect.

questions.q: The intermolecular force of attraction between molecules of the same substance is called——— Force

options.E: None or More than one

questions.explanation: Refer to Q. No. 11

questions.q: The absorption of ink by blotting paper involves

options.C: Diffusion of ink through the blotting paper

questions.explanation: Because blotting paper is a thin, smooth paper that allows thicker liquid-like ink to absorb on its surface, the ink absorption by the paper is based on the capillary action phenomenon. Capillary Action: Capillary action is the liquid's ability to flow in narrow spaces without the assistance of, or even in opposition to, external forces. Capillaries are narrow cylindrical tubes with extremely small diameters, opened at both ends. Info Bits: The tendency of a liquid to rise or fall as a result of surface tension in the capillary tube is known as capillarity. Liquids which wet the glass, rise up and liquids which does not wet the glass, fall down in the capillary tube. For example - when the capillary tube is kept stand in mercury, the mercury surface falls down in tube in comparison to the surface of external mercury. Some examples of capillarity: Water and minerals absorbed by plant roots reaches to leaves and twigs through xylem tissues - which act as capillary tubes. Drawing of ink to the nib from bottom in a fountain pen. The nib is slitted in the middle, which acts as a capillary tube. However, refill pen writing is possible by gravity and it does not depend upon surface tension. Lighting candles and lamp wicks.

questions.q: On a clean glass plate a drop of water spreads to form a thin layer whereas a drop of mercury remains almost spherical because

options.B: The density of mercury is greater than that of water.
options.C: cohesion of mercury is greater than its adhesion with glass.
options.D: cohesion of water is greater than its adhesion with glass.

questions.explanation: The relative strength of the cohesive and adhesive forces acting on a liquid when placed on a smooth surface, such as a glass plate, determines the shape that liquid will take. If the adhesive forces between a liquid and a surface are stronger, they will pull the liquid down, causing it to wet the surface. The liquid will, however, maintain its spherical shape and form beads on the surface if the cohesive forces inside the liquid are stronger and can resist such adherence. Mercury drop remains spherical on a plate of glass because its cohesive force is greater than its adhesive force with glass. Note: A drop of water spreads on the glass as the adhesive force acting on the water(between water and glass) is stronger than the cohesive force among the water molecules.

questions.q: It is more comfortable to wear white clothes in summer because of

options.A: They reflect the heat falling on them.
options.B: They radiate heat transferred from the body.
options.D: They are soothing to the eye.

questions.explanation: We prefer white clothes in summer because white clothes reflect most of the sun's heat and absorb very little of the sun's heat and keep our bodies cool. While black clothes absorb most of the heat that falls on them. Note: Seeing the above phenomenon the best colour of an umbrella in summer shall be white. But, an umbrella with outer black colour and inner surface painted with silver colour is usually preferred in summer because black umbrella absorbs the heat while white colour reflects the heat radiation. Thus, if we use white colour umbrella the heat rays will get deviated and can come down us. While the black colour umbrella will absorb the heat rays and will prevent heat rays from reaching to us. Hence, black colour umbrella is preferred in summer to white colour umbrella.

questions.q: A piece of ice is floating in the beaker containing water. When the whole ice melts.

options.A: The level of water will come down
options.B: The level of water will come up
options.C: The level of water will first rise and then fall
options.D: The level of water will remain the same.

questions.explanation: Suppose an ice cube is floating (in equilibrium) in the water, it will have to displace enough water to support its weight. Now, when the ice has melted, it turns into a volume of water, the same volume as much ice had displaced before. Note: When a piece of ice floating in the glass containing alcohol melts, the level of alcohol in glass comes down because as ice being denser than alcohol will sink and upon melting to water its density increases(density of water > density of ice). Since mass remains the same and density increases , the volume will decrease and thus the level of alcohol in glass will come down.

questions.q: In a railway track, two successive rails are joined end-to-end with a gap in between them, because

options.B: accidents due to contraction in winter can be avoided
options.C: Air-gaps are necessary for bearing the weight of running train
options.D: accidents due to expansion of rails in summer can be avoided

questions.explanation: The rails of the railway track are formed of steel. While laying the railway track, a small gap is left between the two successive lengths of rails. The purpose is that the rails expand in summer due to heat. The gap is fitted to enable this expansion. If no gap is left, the expansion in summer will cause the rails to bend sideways and result in a train accident.

questions.q: When hot liquid is poured into a thick glass tumbler, it cracks because the glass

options.A: is a bad conductor of heat so only inner surface of tumbler expands
options.C: has a very low specific heat

questions.explanation: When hot water is poured into thick glass tumbler, the inner surface of the tumbler becomes hot and expands, however the outer surface of the tumbler does not expand quickly, as Glass is a poor conductor of heat. As heat can't pass from the inner surface of the thick glass into the outer surface quickly. As a result, unequal expansion occurs and causes the tumbler to crack.

questions.explanation: Infrared Waves: Discovered by Herschel Produces heating effect Used to treat cancer, muscle ache and tension. Also used in cookers for cooking and in thermal imaging cameras UV rays was discovered by Ritter UV rays used in preserving foodstuffs, detecting invisible writings and forged documents

questions.q: To diagnose medical conditions, the MRI technique is used. Which is not used in MRI?

options.E: None of the above/More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Magnetic resonance technique (MRI) used in radiology for three dimensional images. It provides detailed images of soft tissue & organs in our body. Ionizing radiation of X-rays is not used in MRI. MRI used to detect brain injury, stroke, brain tumors, disorder of eye and inner ear, etc. MRI is often called a "Non-Invasive" technique as it does not use any ionizing radiation. It only uses a strong magnetic field and radio waves to map the problems. Wilhelm Roentgen, Professor of Physics discovered X-rays in 1895 accidentally while testing whether cathode rays could pass through glass. His cathode tube was covered in heavy black paper, so he was surprised when an incandescent green light nevertheless escaped and projected onto a nearby fluorescent screen. Through experimentation, he found that the mysterious light would pass through most substances but leave shadows of solid objects. Because he did not know what the rays were, he called them 'X' meaning 'unknown' rays.

questions.q: Compound tomography (CT) scan uses
questions.explanation: Compound tomography (CT) scan uses X-ray to produce medical imaging used for medical diagnostics. Through application of this technique, a three-dimensional (3D) image of the patient is formed which shows detailed images of any part of the body, including the bones, muscles, organs, blood vessels and also facilitates the easier identification of possible tumors or abnormalities.

questions.q: The first man who placed his foot on the moon is

options.E: None of the above/More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Apollo 11 was the American spaceflight that first landed humans on the Moon. The Apollo 11 mission was sent on July 16, 1969, by NASA. The President of the USA at that time was John F Kennedy. Neil Armstrong, Edwin "Buzz" Aldrin, and Michael Collins were the astronauts on Apollo 11. On July 20, 1969, Neil Armstrong became the first human to step on the moon. Soon after Aldrin followed him. Note: Yuri Gagarin of the USSR was the first man to go into Space.

questions.q: First Indian satellite 'Aryabhatta' was launched in which year?
questions.explanation: Aryabhatta spacecraft was India's first satellite named after India's famous astronomer. It was completely conceived and designed in India and was launched by a Soviet Kosmos-3M rocket from Kapustin Yar (Volgograd Launch Station, presently in Russia) on April 19, 1975. The name of the launch vehicle was C-1 Intercosmos. It was an experimental type satellite with a mission life of nearly seventeen years (active till March 1981). It re-entered the earth's atmosphere on February 10, 1992. As a mark of celebration of this event by the Reserve Bank of India, the picture of Aryabhatta satellite appeared on the currency note of Rs 2 in between 1976 to 1997.

questions.q: What is TERLS?

options.C: A space science and technology centre
options.D: A satellite launched by the USA

questions.explanation: TERLS stands for “Thumba Equatorial Rocket Launching Station”. It is located in Thumba near Thiruvananthapuram. Scientific investigation of the upper atmosphere and ionosphere were conducted from thumba during early 1960s using small sounding rockets because the geomagnetic equator passes over this place. Info Bits: Vikram Sarabhai Space center (VSSC) Thiruvananthapuram has played a pioneering role in sounding rocket launches for scientific studies. One among the large number of sounding rockets developed and launched from TERLS is 'Rohini series' (RH-200, RH-300, RH-560, etc) which is developed by ISRO. On July 15, 2015, RH-200(number denotes the diameter of the rocket in mm) rocket was launched successfully for the 100th consecutive time from TERLS. The most important campaigns taken up by RH-200 were MONEX→ (Monsoon Experiment), EWS→ (Equatorial Wave Studies), and MIDAS→ (Dynamics Middle Atmosphere). Sounding rockets→ These are one or two stage solid propellant rockets that are used for studying the upper atmospheric region and for space research. They also serve as easily affordable platforms to test or prove prototypes of new components or subsystems intended for use in launch vehicles and satellites. Geomagnetic equator→ it is midway between the geomagnetic poles. It can be defined as an imaginary line on the earth's surface, its plan passing through the center of the earth. It is not fixed, but changes slowly. Geographical equator and geomagnetic equator are not the same. - We know that earth acts like a large spherical magnet which means Earth is surrounded by a magnetic field which is generated by a dipole magnet located at the center of the earth. The magnetic field of the earth changes with time and location. The axis of the earth's dipole differs from the earth's axis of rotation by 11 degrees. Thus, we conclude that north and south geographic poles are not same as the north and south magnetic poles and so is the case for equators.

questions.q: Einstein got the Nobel Prize for

options.E: None of the above/More than one of the above

questions.explanation: Albert Einstein received the Nobel Prize in physics in the year 1921 for his work on the Photoelectric effect. Info Bits: Photoelectric Effect: It states that when light shines on a metal, electrons can be ejected from the surface of the metal. This process is also known as photoemission. The electrons that are ejected from the metal are called photoelectrons. Einstein proposed that light behaved like a stream of particles called photons with an energy of E=hν, where h is Plank's constant and ν is frequency and E is energy. The photoelectric effect was discovered by Heinrich Hertz but was explained by Einstein. Light has dual nature i.e. wave and particle nature, the phenomenon of the photoelectric effect can be explained by treating light as a particle. Phenomenon like interference, diffraction, and polarization can be explained by treating light as a wave. Light travels in a straight line because of its particle nature.

questions.q: A photoelectric cell is a device which

options.A: converts light energy into electric energy
options.B: converts electrical energy into light energy

questions.explanation: A Photoelectric cell is an electronic device which converts light energy into electrical energy. It is also called an electric eye or photo tube. It works on the principle of photoelectric effect. It is used for sound reproduction in a motion picture, in burglar alarms etc. The photoelectric effect is the emission of electrons or other free carriers when light shines on a material. Electrons emitted in this way are called photoelectrons. The photoelectric effect was first introduced by Wilhelm Ludwig Franz Hallwachs in the year 1887, and the experimental verification was done by Heinrich Rudolf Hertz.

questions.q: In which year, Sir C.V. Raman was awarded the Nobel Prize for Raman Effect?
questions.explanation: In the year 1930, Sir Chandrasekhara Venkat Raman was awarded the Nobel Prize for Raman Effect in Physics. February 28 is celebrated as National Science Day in India, as the Raman Effect was discovered on this day in 1928. He was also awarded Bharat Ratna in 1954 and was the first scientist to receive this honour.

questions.q: Which of the following photoelectric devices is most suitable for digital applications?

options.D: More than one of the above
options.E: None of the above

questions.explanation: Photovoltaic cell is the photoelectric device which is most suitable for digital applications such as satellite communications. Photoelectric effect: It is an instantaneous process in which ejection of photoelectrons takes place when a beam of light is incident on a metal surface. Photoelectric effect was first discovered by Heinrich Hertz in 1887. But it was Albert Einstein who proved the particle nature of the light (light has momentum) and thus explained the Photoelectric effect and got the Nobel Prize for the same in 1921. A photovoltaic cell is a technological application of the photoelectric effect. It produces electricity or electric signals when a light ray is incident on its surface. Hence, option C is the right answer. Info Bits: Photodiode shows variation in its electric properties when exposed to a light source. When connected in a circuit it acts like a light controlled switch that allows electricity to flow on getting light. Electricity is not getting generated through a light source hence; it is not a photoelectric device. Photoemitter, which is also called a light emitting diode, emits light when connected to a voltage source i.e., when electricity is passing through it. Thus, the effect is the reverse of the photoelectric effect. Hence, it is also not a photoelectric device.

questions.q: A sudden rise in Barometric Height indicates

options.E: None of the above/More than one of the above

questions.explanation: The Barometer was invented by Evangelista Torricelli in 1643. It is used to measure atmospheric pressure. A high reading on the barometer means high atmospheric pressure, which meteorologists predict as good weather, while a low barometer value means low atmospheric pressure which is indicative of poor weather. When the air is dry, cool, and pleasant, the barometer reading rises. In general, a rising barometer means improving weather. In general, a falling barometer means worsening weather. When atmospheric pressure drops suddenly, it means pressure has decreased outside this usually indicates that a storm is on its way. When atmospheric pressure remains steady, there will likely be no immediate change in the weather.

questions.q: The scientist who first discovered that the earth revolves around the sun was
questions.explanation: In 1543, Nicolaus Copernicus detailed his radical theory of the Universe in which the Earth, along with the other planets, rotated around the Sun. John Dalton introduced the Atomic Theory in 1808 which is also known as the billiard ball model. Newton's laws of motion- - The first law is the law of inertia. - Newton's second law is the law of force and acceleration. - The third law is also known as the law of action and reaction. Note: In 1905 Albert Einstein developed a mass energy equation which is usually referred to as E=mc².

questions.q: Passengers in a moving bus are thrown forward when the driver suddenly applies brakes. This can be explained

options.A: Through Newton's first law of motion
options.B: Through Newton's second law of motion
options.C: Through Newton's third law of motion
options.D: By the principle of conservation of momentum

questions.explanation: The first law of motion is stated as 'An object remains in a state of rest or of uniform motion in a straight line unless compelled to change that state by an applied force'. The first law of motion is also known as the law of inertia. Passengers tend to remain at rest with respect to the seat until the driver applies a braking force to stop the motorcar. With the application of brakes, the car slows down but the body tends to continue in the same state of motion because of its inertia. Thus, passengers in a moving bus are thrown forward when the driver suddenly applies brakes.

questions.q: A bus is moving along a straight path and takes a sharp turn to the right side suddenly. The passengers sitting in the bus will

options.D: More than one of the above
options.E: None of the above

questions.explanation: We have often observed that when a bus is moving along a straight path takes a sharp turn to the right side suddenly, the passengers sitting in the bus will bent towards the left side. This Phenomenon can be explained by applying Newton's First Law of motion which is also known as law of inertia which states that a body at rest will remain at rest or a body in motion will remain in motion until and unless an external force is applied to change its state. Info Bits: When a passenger is sitting in the bus the lower part of his body is in contact with the bus which follows the direction of the motion of the bus due to which the whole body is following the same direction of motion as that of the bus and when the bus takes a sharp turn to right the lower part of the body will also try to follow the same direction of motion as it is in the contact with the bus but the upper body is still maintaining the inertia which means it is following the straight direction of motion as in the earlier case. As a result, the passenger will bend towards the opposite direction i.e., towards the left side.

questions.q: A goalkeeper in a game of football pulls his hands backwards after holding the ball, shot at the goal. This enables goalkeeper to

options.A: increase the force exerted by the ball on hands
options.B: decrease the rate of change of momentum
options.C: exert large force on the ball
options.D: More than one of the above
options.E: None of the above

questions.explanation: A goalkeeper in a game of football pulls his hands backwards after holding the ball shot at the goal. This enables the goalkeeper to decrease the rate of change of momentum, which means he has to exert less force and will not get hurt while catching that ball. Info Bits: Force not only depends on the change in momentum but also upon the rate at which the change of momentum is being carried out. It is observed that while catching a ball in cricket the seasoned cricketer draws in his hand (pulls his hand backward), it allows greater time for the ball to stop and hence, requires a smaller force. In this case the change in the momentum of the ball is carried out at a much slower rate thus the player has to exert a lesser amount of force and he does not get hurt. If a player keeps his hand fixed at a position and tries to stop or catch the ball almost instantly. The change in the momentum of the ball in this case is bought at a faster rate thus the player has to exert greater force to catch that ball and, in the process, gets hurt as well.

questions.q: Forces of action and reaction are

options.D: Always equal and in the same direction

questions.explanation: Forces of action and reaction are equal in magnitude and opposite in direction. Info Bits: According to Newton's third law of motion when one object applies a force on another object (force of action) the second object also applies a force equal in magnitude and opposite in direction on the first object instantaneously (force of reaction). The examples are firing of a Gun (when we apply force on a gun in forward direction it recoils in the opposite direction), jumping out of a sailor from a rowing boat (when the person jumps in forward direction the boat moves in the backward direction), etc. Note: Force of action and reaction acts on two different bodies at the same time.

questions.q: Which of the following can cause cancer as well as cure it depending upon its intensity and use?
questions.explanation: Ionized radiation can cause cancer as well as cure it. Radiation is energy that moves from one place to another in a form that can be described as waves or particles. Radiation can occur naturally or be man-made. There are two types of radiation: Ionizing radiation and Non-ionizing radiation, - Ionizing radiation is a type of radiation of such energy that it can detach electrons from atoms or molecules, which causes changes at the atomic level when interacting with matter including living organisms which includes radio waves, cell phones, microwaves, infrared radiation and visible light. - Non-ionizing radiation is lower energy radiation that is not energetic enough to detach electrons from atoms or molecules, whether in matter or living organisms ultraviolet radiation, radon, x-rays, and gamma rays, ionizing radiation has enough energy to damage DNA and cause cancer Info Bits: Radiation therapy, also called radiation oncology, radiotherapy, or therapeutic radiology, is the use of ionizing radiation (high-energy radiation that displaces electrons from atoms and molecules) to destroy cancer cells. Tobacco: A plant with leaves that have high levels of the addictive chemical nicotine.

questions.q: Alpha radiation is not used in radiotherapy, because

options.B: It is unable to penetrate human skin
options.C: It is toxic to human tissues

questions.explanation: High doses of radiation are used in radiation therapy, also known as radiotherapy, as a cancer treatment to eradicate cancer cells and reduce tumor size. Alpha particle is a weak penetrating type of particle radiation. That is why they are not applied in radiotherapy because they cannot penetrate human skin. X-rays, Y-rays. Electron beams or protons are used for destroying or damaging cancer cells.

questions.q: Which among the following is a positively charged particle emitted by a radioactive element?

options.D: More than one of the above
options.E: None of the above

questions.explanation: Alpha ray is a positively charged particle emitted by a radioactive element. Radioactivity was discovered by A. H Becquerel in 1896. In radioactivity an unstable nucleus undergoes a decay. There are three types of radioactive decay namely, α – decay, β – decay, γ – decay. - In α – decay, helium is emitted. In α – decay → $X_Z^A$ decays into $X_{Z-2}^{A-4} + He_2^4$ . - The disintegration energy, (Q) = difference between the initial mass energy and the total mass energy of the decay product. And Q for exothermic process like α - decay is greater than zero. - Example of α – decay → Decay of Uranium $(U_{92}^{238} \rightarrow U_{90}^{234} + He_2^4)$

questions.q: If an inflated balloon, is brought near lighted bulb, it bursts because

options.C: Air Inside the balloon becomes hot and puts pressure on the wall of the balloon
options.E: None or More

questions.explanation: A lighted-bulb produces heat, when a balloon is brought near a light bulb, the heating effect caused by the bulb heats molecules in the balloon to vibrate, which causes thermal expansion in the size of the balloon. This expansion in size causes bursting.

questions.q: It is more difficult to walk on a sandy road than on a concrete road, because

options.A: Sand is soft and concrete is hard
options.B: The friction between sand and feet is less than that between concrete and feet.
options.C: The friction between sand and feet is more than that between concrete and feet.
options.D: Sand is grainy but concrete is smooth.

questions.explanation: Sand reduces friction. Its particles are small and irregular and they are not attached to each other or anything. If we try to walk on a sandy road, it glides over easily, offering no resistance at all.

questions.q: Ball bearings are used in cycles / scooters etc. to

options.A: Increase the friction between the wheel and the axle
options.B: Reduce the friction between the wheel and the axle
options.C: Reduce the friction between the ground the wheel

questions.explanation: Ball bearing reduces friction by making the two surfaces (axle and wheel) roll over each other. This happens due to the rolling action of small metal balls present inside the ball bearing.

questions.q: Ball bearings are used in bicycles, cars, etc., because

options.A: The actual area of contact between the wheel and axle is increased
options.B: The effective area of contact between the wheel and axle is increased
options.C: The effective area of contact between the wheel and axle is reduced
options.D: None of the above statements is correct

questions.explanation: Ball bearings are used in bicycles, cars, etc., to reduce the area of contact between the wheel and axle which in turn reduces friction. Info Bits: Frictional force comes into picture whenever there is a relative motion between two bodies or surfaces in contact and it acts on both surfaces. Ball bearing enables the body to roll over the surface of another body and the friction involved in rolling i.e., the rolling friction is always less than the sliding friction which makes the movement or motion easier. Hence, option C is correct.

questions.q: Ball bearings are used to convert static friction into

questions.q: Which of the following energy changes involves frictional forces?

options.D: More than one of the above
options.E: None of the above

questions.explanation: Frictional Forces account for the change in the form of energy from Kinetic to Thermal or Heat. Hence, option B is the correct answer. Info Bits: Kinetic Energy is the form of energy possessed by a body of mass 'm' moving with a velocity 'v' and its magnitude is calculated by the formula, K.E = ½ (mv²). One of the most common examples is when we rub our hands in winters, we feel warmth as the kinetic energy of our hand in motion gets converted to thermal energy. Another example can be the lighting of fire by rubbing two stones or the catching of fire of racing cars in Formula 1 racing where the tires of the wheels rub against the road and experience the force of friction which converts some of the kinetic energy of racing cars into thermal energy. The core principle involved in this is the law of conservation of energy which states that energy can neither be created nor destroyed, it can be transformed from one form to another i.e., total energy of an isolated system remains constant.

questions.q: Inside an Aeroplane, flying at a high altitude.

options.A: the pressure is the same as that of outside
options.B: normal atmospheric pressure is maintained by the use of air pumps
options.C: the pressure inside is less than the pressure outside
options.D: normal humidity and partial vacuum are maintained.

questions.explanation: In order to prevent discomfort for passengers due to low outside air pressure, the cabin pressure of an airplane is kept higher than the atmospheric pressure at high altitudes. This pressure is called cabin pressurization. Hence the normal atmospheric pressure is maintained by the use of air pumps.

questions.q: Which one of the following types of laser is used in laser printer?
questions.explanation: Printer: It is an output device and is used to print characters, images and symbols on paper. Laser printers: - These printers produce high quality paper prints at a high speed. On an average 6 to 12 pages can be printed per minute using these printers. It is almost quiet (noiseless in operation) and uses dry magnetic ink technology to print. - In laser printers a semiconductor laser is used. Typically, aluminum-gallium-arsenide (AlGaAs) lasers are used in laser printers. Semiconductor lasers are used in laser printers because they are small in size and are lightweight, have long life and low power consumption, etc. Hence, option C is correct.

questions.q: Which statement about LEDs (Light- Emitting Diodes) is not correct?

options.A: LED light sources consume less electricity
options.B: LEDs have longer lifetime than light bulbs.
options.C: LEDs are available in colors such as red, green, yellow, blue and white.
options.D: LEDs use mercury which is harmful for humans.

questions.explanation: LEDs (Light Emitting Diodes) can be defined as semiconductor devices which produce light when connected to a forward bias voltage source. In LEDS a heavily doped (adding of impurities to enhance the conductivity of a pure semiconductor) p-n junction diode is used. The meaning of forward bias means positive-terminal of the battery is connected to the p-type semiconductor of the diode and the negative terminal of the battery is connected to the n-type semiconductor of the diode. Features of LEDs: - LEDs operate on a lower voltage thus consume less power. - They operate faster than the conventional incandescent low power lamps and do not require any warm-up time so they light instantly (almost in nanoseconds). - The light emitted by LEDs is nearly monochromatic (single wavelength light). - It has a long life. - It has a fast on-off switching capability. - It is not affected by low temperatures and will light up in subzero cold weather as well. - It is available in various colors such as red, green, yellow, blue and white. The compound semiconductor Gallium Arsenide-Phosphide in different proportions is used to make LEDs of different colors. Hence, option D is not a correct statement as LEDs do not contain mercury.

questions.q: The known forces of nature can be divided into four classes, viz. gravity, electromagnetism, weak nuclear force and strong nuclear force. With reference to them, which one of the following statements is not correct?

options.A: Gravity is the strongest of the four
options.B: Electromagnetism acts only on particles with an electric charge
options.D: Strong nuclear force holds protons and neutrons inside the nucleus of an atom

questions.explanation: | Force | Definition | Relative strength | Range | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | Gravitational force | It is a universal force, which means every object experiences it due to every other object. It can be defined as the force of mutual attraction between any two objects by virtue of their masses. It is always attractive. It is responsible for the motion of the moon around the earth, motion of the earth, planets around the sun, motion of artificial satellites around the earth Motion of the bodies falling to the earth. It also plays a key role in formation and evolution of stars, galaxies, etc. | 10⁻³⁹ | Infinite | | Weak nuclear force | It appears only in certain nuclear processes for example in the decay of a nucleus. It is stronger than the gravitational force but much weaker than the electromagnetic as well as strong nuclear forces. | 10⁻¹³ | Very short, sub-nuclear size (approx 10⁻¹⁶ m) | | Electromagnetic force | It can be defined as the force between charged particles. It acts over large distances and does not need any intervening medium. This force is much stronger than the gravitational force. It can be attractive as well as repulsive. It governs the structure of atoms and molecules. | 10⁻² | Infinite | | Strong nuclear force | It is the strongest of all fundamental forces (100 times stronger than the electromagnetic force). It can be defined as the force which binds protons and neutrons in a nucleus. It is an attractive force. questions.q: The meter that is used to measure the distance moved by the vehicle is known as
questions.explanation: Odometer - An odometer measures the total distance traveled by a vehicle. - It is usually located on the vehicle's dashboard. - The unit of measurement is generally kilometers (km) or miles. Thus, odometer is the correct answer. Chronometer - A chronometer is a very precise timekeeping device, often used in navigation (especially in ships). It measures time, not distance. Speedometer - A speedometer measures the instantaneous speed of a moving vehicle (e.g., 60 km/h). It does not measure distance - only speed. Ammeter - An ammeter measures electric current in a circuit. It is completely unrelated to distance or vehicles.

questions.q: The number of scientific payloads onboard the Aditya-L1 mission for the systematic study of the Sun is
questions.explanation: The Aditya-L1 mission is India's first dedicated solar mission, launched by ISRO (Indian Space Research Organisation). It aims to study various aspects of the Sun, especially the solar corona, solar wind, magnetic fields, and solar flares. - Launch vehicle: PSLV-C57. - Mission type: Solar observation - Destination: Lagrange Point 1 (L1) - about 1.5 million km from Earth toward the Sun It carries 7 scientific payloads, categorized as: 4 payloads to observe the Sun directly (remote sensing) and 3 for in-situ study of particles and magnetic fields. Key Goals - Study of the solar corona - the outermost layer of the Sun, visible during eclipses. - Understand coronal mass ejections (CMEs) - huge explosions of solar plasma that affect Earth's magnetosphere. - Study the solar wind and its composition. - Observe the Sun's magnetic field variations. - Understand the heating mechanism of the solar corona. - Help in space weather prediction, which is crucial for protecting satellites and astronauts. | Instrument | Function | | --- | --- | | VELC (Visible Emission Line Coronagraph) | Observes the solar corona and CME origins. | | SUIT (Solar Ultraviolet Imaging Telescope) | Images the Sun in the near UV spectrum. | | ASPEX (Aditya Solar wind Particle Experiment) | Measures solar wind particles. | | PAPA (Plasma Analyser Package for Aditya) | Analyzes particle energy distribution. | | SoLEXS (Solar Low Energy X-ray Spectrometer) | Studies X-ray flares from the Sun. | | HEL1OS (High Energy L1 Orbiting X-ray Spectrometer) | Detects high-energy solar emissions. | | MAG (Magnetometer) | Measures the magnetic field at L1. |

questions.q: In the context of lab-grown diamonds (LGDs), what is used as a diamond seed?
questions.explanation: - LGDs are diamonds that are produced using specific technology which mimics the geological processes that grow natural diamonds. They are produced in two main methods - CVD (Chemical Vapor Deposition) & HPHT (High Pressure High Temperature). - During CVD diamond creation, a minuscule slice of diamond is placed into a chamber where it is exposed to carbon-rich gas and amped up to extremely high temperatures. Over a matter of just weeks, the carbon gas ionizes and the particles stick to the original diamond slice, before eventually crystallizing into a fully formed diamond. - During HPHT, pure carbon is pressed within a metal cube and exposed to immense heat and pressure through electric pulses. Eventually, the carbon breaks down and crystallizes into a diamond. Any metal traces within a HPHT diamond will be minuscule and usually not visible to the naked eye. - Most experts agree that HPHT diamonds are better quality than CVD diamonds. - Usually graphite is used as a diamond seed and when subjected to extreme conditions it turns into one of the most expensive carbon forms i.e. diamond. Catalysts such as chromium, Iron or platinum may be used. - India is the largest producer of lab grown diamonds through the CVD technology and contributes to nearly 25% of global LGD production. - Materials like Moissanite, Cubic Zirconia (CZ), White Sapphire, YAG (Yttrium aluminium garnet) etc are diamond stimulants that simply attempt to look like a diamond but they lack the sparkle and durability of a diamond as are thus easily identifiable.

questions.q: Which of the following technologies will be enabled by the 5G mobile communication networks?
questions.explanation: 5G is the fifth generation of wireless cellular technology, offering higher upload and download speeds, more consistent connections, and improved capacity than previous networks. It has the potential to transform the way we use the internet to access applications, social networks, and information. For example, technologies like self-driving cars, advanced gaming applications, and live streaming media that require very reliable, high-speed data connections are set to benefit greatly from 5G connectivity. The capabilities of 5G can support innovation and improve customer experiences for business. Here are some areas to look out for. - Internet of Things: The Internet of Things (IoT) refers to a network of physical devices, vehicles, appliances and other physical objects that are embedded with sensors, software and network connectivity that allows them to collect and share data. The commercial success of any IoT is tied to its performance, which is dependent on how quickly it can communicate with other IoT devices, smartphones and tablets, software in the form of its app or website, and more. With 5G, data-transfer speeds will increase significantly. 5G will be 10 times faster than current LTE (Long-term Evolution) networks. This increase in speed will allow IoT devices to communicate and share data faster than ever. - Edge computing: Edge computing is the process of delivering data storage and analysis capabilities closer to your endpoints. With the ever-increasing quality of edge computing, using cases and data requirements, a high-speed network is necessary to satisfy the need for near real-time responsiveness. As such, 5G network infrastructure supports and enables the increasing complexity and specialization of edge computing. - Network slicing: Network slicing is a method of creating multiple unique logical and virtualized networks over a common multi-domain infrastructure. Mobile network operators use 5G technology to deploy multiple independent virtual networks over the same infrastructure. You can customize each network slice for different services and business cases, such as streaming services or enterprise tasks. By forming a collection of 5G network functions for each specific use case or business model, you can support different requirements from all vertical industries. The service separation means users benefit from a more reliable experience and improved efficiency on their devices. Info Bits: 5G is the fifth generation mobile network. It is the next generation cellular technology that will provide faster and more reliable communication with ultra-low latency. With 5G, the peak network data speeds are expected to be in the range of 2-20 Gigabit per second (Gbps) as opposed to 4G link speeds averaging 6-7 Megabit per second (Mbps) in India.

questions.q: Match List- I with List - II:
questions.explanation: - Cassini-Huygens: The Cassini-Huygens project was a cooperative project between NASA, ESA (European Space Agency) and the ASI (Italian Space Agency). NASA supplied the main spacecraft, the orbiter Cassini, and ESA supplied the lander, Huygens. It was sent to study Saturn and its complex system of rings and moons. Launched on 15 October 1997, the Huygens probe descended into the atmosphere of Titan, Saturn's largest moon, on 14 January 2005. Cassini orbited Saturn from 2004 to 2017 and the mission ended on Sept.15,2017. The Huygens was named after the Dutch physicist Christiaan Huygens (1629-1695). Cassini was named after Italian astronomer Giovanni Cassini (1625-1712). - Juno: Juno is a space probe by NASA that is currently orbiting the planet Jupiter. Launched on 5th August 2011, it entered Jupiter's orbit on 4 July 2016 to probe beneath Jupiter's dense clouds and study its origins, structure, atmosphere and magnetosphere. The mission is named after goddess Juno. In Roman mythology, it's said that the mighty god Jupiter would cloak himself in clouds to hide his mischievous deeds. Only his wife, the goddess Juno, could peer through the shroud and see his true self. - Artemis: NASA's Artemis missions aim to land the first woman and first person of color on the Moon, explore the lunar surface, and lay the groundwork for sending astronauts to Mars. This 'Moon to Mars' plan involves building a new space station in lunar orbit and, eventually, establishing a habitable Moon base. The name of the mission is derived from "Artemis", the mythological Greek goddess of the Moon - and twin sister of Apollo. The name is intended to link this programme with the Apollo missions that first landed humans on the Moon over 50 years ago. The crewed spacecraft, meanwhile, is called Orion. Orion is the name of one of the most recognizable constellations in the sky. In Classical mythology, Orion is the hunting companion of Artemis. - VERITAS: VERITAS is an acronym for "Venus Emissivity, Radio Science, InSAR, Topography, and Spectroscopy." The word Veritas means "truth" in Latin, and the mission will reveal the truth of how Venus' and Earth's paths diverged. It aims to map the surface of the planet Venus in high resolution and also to help scientists understand how Venus became an inhospitable inferno, while Earth evolved to become home to an abundance of life. It is an upcoming mission from NASA's Jet Propulsion Laboratory (LPL) and is currently planned to launch in December 2027.

questions.q: Which of the following are computer languages?
questions.explanation: - Cobra is a high-level, object oriented programming language used for general programming purposes. Its main selling points are quick, expressive coding, fast execution, static and dynamic binding and language level support for quality. questions.q: What is the full form of GPT in ChatGPT, recently seen in the news?
questions.explanation: - ChatGPT, which stands for Chat Generative Pre-trained Transformer, is an AI-powered (Artificial intelligence) language model developed by OpenAI (an AI and research company) capable of generating human-like text based on context and past conversations. - It was launched on November 30, 2022. - The language model can answer questions and assist a person with tasks, such as composing emails, essays, and code. - Sam Altman is the current Chief Executive Officer (CEO) of OpenAI.

questions.q: What is/are the full form(s) of HMX?
questions.explanation: - HMX (Octogen) is an acronym for High Melting Explosive. - It is also known as octogen and cyclotetramethylene-tetranitramine, as well as by other names. - It is a colorless solid that dissolves slightly in water. Only a small amount of HMX (Octogen) will evaporate into the air; however, it can occur in air attached to suspended particles or dust. - HMX (Octogen) does not occur naturally in the environment. It is made from other chemicals known as hexamine, ammonium nitrate, nitric acid, and acetic acid. - HMX (Octogen) explodes violently at high temperatures. Because of this property, HMX (Octogen) is used in various kinds of explosives, rocket fuels, and burster chargers.

questions.q: What is the name of Tesla's humanoid robot launched in 2022?
questions.explanation: Optimus or Tesla bot is a humanoid robot launched by Tesla in 2022. It is a general-purpose bot designed to perform tasks that are unsafe, repetitive, or boring. It would be low cost and will be available to the public sometime between 2025 and 2027.

Electric Current and Conductivity: - Electric current refers to the rate at which an electric charge moves across a conductor. - Positive charge flow is regarded as the direction of electric current flow. - Electric currents are of two types: direct current and alternating current. - The main benefit of alternating current over direct current is that it can be transmitted across great distances with little electric loss. - The SI unit of electric power is watt but for commercial purposes kilowatt-hour is used which is popularly known as unit. - 1 unit = 1 kilowatt hour = 1000 watt hour. - Value of 1 unit is 3.6 × 10⁶ Joule. - No heat is lost when electrical energy is transformed into motion. - The electromagnetic induction theory underlies the operation of electric motors and dynamos. - When working at low voltages, electric motors are more likely to burn out as a result of drawing more current, which is inversely proportional to voltage. - Reason: As the Power (P) of motor is constant, and P=VI ie. I=P/V, which means Current is inversely proportional to Voltage, such that when voltage is low, current is high, and this high current causes power loss (I²R) by producing large heat and thus burning the motor. - Faraday constant is a universal constant. - Nickel-cadmium batteries are used in calculators, transistors, flashlights, portable power tools, etc. - Tungsten is used as a filament in lighting bulbs. - The Halogen lamp filament is an alloy of tungsten and sodium, and due to sodium, it emits yellow light. - Fuse wire used for the safety of the house has high resistance and low melting point. - CFL (compact Fluorescent lamp) has a lifespan of about 6000 and 10000 hours and emits light using mercury vapour and phosphor. - LED lamps are more energy-efficient and have a lifespan of roughly 50000 hours. They produce light using semiconductor material. - LED works on the principle of electroluminescence. - Fluorescent tubes are filled with noble gases such as argon, neon, and xenon, as well as low-pressure gases like mercury. - Nitrogen and argon gases are filled in the electric bulbs, to prevent the oxidation of Tungsten filament used in the bulb. - Parallel connections are used in home electrical wiring. - White light is produced by oscillating the molecules in the tube. - A colour code for wires is used in wiring to avoid confusion viz. black for neutral, red for live wire, and green for earth wire. - Transformers are used for step-up or step-down AC voltages. - Mobile phone charger is a step-down transformer. - AC current is not preferable to charge storage batteries. - AC current is converted into DC current using a rectifier. - Germanium and silicon are used as semiconductors in transistors. - Silicons are used in solar cells or photovoltaic cells. - Silver, copper, aluminum, and iron are the best conductor of electricity as they are the most electrically conductive element. - Zero permeability is the characteristic feature of superconductors. - Ceramic oxides viz... Mercury, calcium, barium, and thallium, are newly discovered high-temperature superconductors. - Resistance of semiconductors decreases with the increase in temperature and at absolute zero temperature the electric resistance in semiconductors is infinite. Nuclear Physics - Photons, deuterons, and alpha particles are accelerated by cyclotrons. - The ultimate source of energy in stars and the sun is hydrogen which changes into helium due to nuclear fusion. - Uranium 235 or plutonium 239 are used as fuel in nuclear reactors, previously known as atomic piles or molecular furnaces," and when these fissile atomic nuclei absorb a neutron, they may undergo nuclear fission. - With the release of kinetic energy, the heavy nucleus is divided into two or more lighter nuclei, free neutrons as well as gamma rays. - H₂O (light water), D₂O (heavy water), graphite, or beryllium oxide are used as moderators to slow down the neutrons in this reaction, which is also known as a self-sustaining chain reaction. - As a nuclear fuel, radioactive elements such as thorium, plutonium, and uranium are used. - The principle of an atomic bomb is based on the uncontrolled nuclear fission of uranium while a hydrogen bomb is based upon the uncontrolled nuclear fusion of deuterium and tritium (isotopes of hydrogen). - The nuclear doctrine of India contains no first use, unilateral moratorium, and minimum credible deterrence.
